concerns.text: FR-001 and FR-009 mandate ingesting 'Last.fm 1-Billion Listening Events' and joining it with MusicBrainz. The tasks (T010-T013) only implement MPD ingestion. While the Plan waives this, the tasks lack a specific task to formally document the spec amendment or handle the 'Last.fm' data path (even if empty), leaving the spec requirement unaddressed in the implementation plan.

FR-006 and SC-003 mandate a sensitivity analysis with a specific threshold sweep {0.005, 0.01, 0.02}. The Plan waives this. Task T036b implements the sweep to 'override' the plan, but Task T039 (Spec Amendment) is listed as a dependency for T036b. This creates a circular dependency where the implementation of the spec requirement is blocked by a governance task that itself depends on the implementation to justify the amendment. The coverage is logically broken.

Tasks T040 and T041 introduce a new streaming architecture (Hugging Face `streaming=True`) not present in the original Plan or Spec. These tasks are tagged as 'Phase O' (Analyze & Resolve) but are not linked to any specific FR/SC in the spec (which assumes batch processing). This creates 'stale tag' confusion where new implementation details exist without a corresponding spec requirement or plan element to anchor them.

The Independent Test for US-1 requires verifying 'a sample of the MPD and Last.fm datasets'. The tasks (T010-T015) only cover MPD. There is no task to generate a 'Last.fm sample' or a mock dataset to satisfy the test condition, meaning the test defined in the spec cannot be executed as written.
